back_translation: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, [writing] to the church of God which is in the city of Corinth, together with all the saints who are throughout the province of Achaia.
May grace and peace from God our Father, and from Jesus Christ the Lord, abide with you all.
Blessed [be] God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who is the Father of all mercies and the God of all comfort/encouragement.
[He] who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we may be able to comfort all those who are in various troubles, with the comfort which we ourselves have received from God.
For just as the suffering of Christ overflows to us, our comfort also overflows in the same way through Christ.

If we are pressed/oppressed with troubles, it is for the comfort and salvation of you; if we receive comfort, it is for your comfort, which produces fruit in the endurance of the same suffering that we ourselves also bear.
And our hope concerning you is firm, because we know that just as you share/participate in the sufferings, you will also share in the comfort in the same way.

Brethren, we do not want you to be unaware about the affliction we encountered in the province of Asia, that we were pressed down heavily beyond [our] strength to bear, until we even despaired of remaining alive.
Indeed in ourselves we received a verdict/sentence to death, so that we would not trust in ourselves, but trust in God who raises the dead to life.
He delivered us from such great peril of death, and will continue to deliver us. We have placed our hope in him that he will deliver us again.
While you also join together in helping us by prayer, so that the gift of grace given to us by means of the prayers of many people will cause many to offer thanksgiving for us.

For the thing that we are proud of is this, namely the testimony of our conscience, that we have conducted our lives in the world, especially toward you, with the holiness and sincerity that comes from God, not with worldly/fleshly wisdom, but with the grace of God.
For we do not write anything to you except what you read and can understand; and I hope that you will understand fully to the end.
Just as you have understood us in part, that we are the cause of your boasting, and you also are the cause of our boasting in the day of our Lord Jesus.
And with this confidence, I had already intended beforehand to come to you, so that you might receive a second [grace/blessing].
Namely passing through you to the province of Macedonia, then returning from Macedonia to you again, then having you send me on to the province of Judea.
Therefore, when I intended this, did I act flightily/capriciously? Or do the things that I plan, I plan according to the flesh, until in me there is both 'Yes, yes' and 'No, no'?
But as God is faithful, our word/message that came to you is not both 'Yes' and 'No' at the same time.
For the Son of God, namely Jesus Christ, whom I, Silvanus, and Timothy proclaimed among you, was not both 'Yes' and 'No,' but 'Yes' has been accomplished/fulfilled in him.
For all the promises of God are 'Yes' in him; therefore through him we say 'Amen' to God for the glory of him.
[It is] God who establishes us together with you firmly in Christ, and has anointed us.
He has placed his seal on us, and has given the Spirit as a deposit/down-payment in our hearts.
But I call God as witness against my life, that out of the desire to spare you, I have not yet returned to the city of Corinth.
Not that we are masters over your faith, but we are co-workers for the joy of you, because you stand firm by faith.
